description: Browse Salesforce org metadata directly from VS Code.
orgBrowser.activitybar.title: Salesforce Org Browser
orgBrowser.view.name: Salesforce Org Browser
orgBrowser.command.toggleLocalFilterOff: Show Local Types (inactive — click to enable)
orgBrowser.command.toggleOrgFilterOff: Show Org Types (inactive — click to enable)
orgBrowser.command.toggleOrgFilterNoData: Org filter unavailable — expand a metadata type first to load org data
orgBrowser.command.clearFilter: Edit Metadata Type Filter (clear to remove)
org_browser_walkthrough_description: See your org and your project in one unified view — with live sync state, smart filtering, and one-click actions.
org_browser_walkthrough_open_description:
The Org Browser shows every metadata type and component — whether it lives in your org, your local project, or both — with live sync status.

**$(pass-filled) Green check** — synced between org and local

**$(arrow-up) Yellow arrow** — modified locally, not yet deployed

**$(arrow-down) Blue arrow** — changed in the org, not yet pulled

**$(warning) Warning** — conflicting changes in both

[Open Org Browser](command:workbench.view.extension.metadata)
org_browser_walkthrough_open_alt: Org Browser sidebar showing metadata types with sync state icons
org_browser_walkthrough_pending_changes_:
The **Pending Changes** section at the top of the tree shows everything that's out of sync — conflicts, remote changes, and local changes — organized and counted.

No CLI commands to run. Conflicts surface here immediately, not when your deploy fails.
Pending Changes section showing conflicts, remote changes, and local changes with counts
org_browser_walkthrough_filter_descripti:
**Four view modes** let you focus on what matters — project types, local only, org only, or all types. Switch with a single click.

**Filter by name or state:** type `ApexClass` to jump to a type, `Layout:Account` for a specific component, or `@conflict` to see all conflicts.

Combine tags freely — `@modified @remote` shows everything that changed in the org since you last synced.

[Filter Metadata Types](command:sf.org-browser.filterTypes)
org_browser_walkthrough_filter_alt: Filter input showing type suggestions and state tag completions
org_browser_walkthrough_sync_description:
Click any component for context-aware actions:

**Retrieve from Org** — pull a remote change

**Deploy to Org** — push a local change

**Preview from Org** — view the org version read-only, without downloading

Bulk actions let you pull all remote changes or deploy all local changes in one click. Create new components directly from the type node with the **+** button.
org_browser_walkthrough_sync_alt: Context menu showing retrieve, deploy, and preview actions on a component
org_browser_walkthrough_custom_object_de:
Expand any **Custom Object** to see its fields with type details — field type, length, scale, precision, and relationship names are shown inline.

This lets you understand your data model directly in VS Code without opening Setup.
org_browser_walkthrough_custom_object_al: Custom object expanded showing fields with type details and sync state
